meta.name: Shawarma Flatbread Wrap
meta.subtitle: Soft, pliable flatbread for shawarma wraps (stovetop) + warm-and-wrap method

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed to be flexible and tear-resistant for shawarma wraps.
Stovetop method for households without a tandoor/oven stone.
Includes optional yogurt version for extra tenderness.

summary: A soft, pliable stovetop flatbread made for shawarma wraps—flexible enough to roll tight without cracking. Includes a fast warm-and-wrap method so your wraps stay tender and sealed.

optional.label: Stand mixer
optional.notes: Optional; speeds kneading but not required.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Approx. 360 g depending on your flour. Use a scale if possible.

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Optional but helps browning and yeast activity.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast
ingredients.notes: If using active dry yeast, bloom it in warm water first.

ingredients.label: Warm water
ingredients.notes: Target 105–110°F (40–43°C). Adjust 1–3 tbsp as needed for a soft dough.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt (optional tenderness version)
ingredients.notes: If using yogurt, reduce water by ~2 tbsp (start with 1 cup water and add as needed).

ingredients.label: Flour for dusting
ingredients.notes: Use sparingly to keep flatbreads tender.

ingredients.label: Oil for skillet (optional)
ingredients.notes: Dry skillet is typical; a light oil film can boost browning on some pans.

steps.title: Mix the dough
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk flour, salt, sugar (if using), and instant yeast.
Add warm water and olive oil. Add yogurt (optional) if using the tenderness version.
Stir until a shaggy dough forms. If it looks dry, add warm water 1 tbsp at a time; if sticky, add flour 1 tbsp at a time.
checks: Dough should be soft and slightly tacky, not wet.

steps.title: Knead until smooth
instructions: Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead 6–8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
If using a mixer: knead on low 4–5 minutes with dough hook until smooth.
Avoid adding too much flour—softer dough makes more pliable wraps.
checks: Dough surface is smooth; springs back slowly when pressed.

steps.title: First rise
instructions: Lightly oil the bowl, place dough inside, and cover.
Rise until doubled, 60–75 minutes in a warm spot (or longer if your kitchen is cool).

steps.title: Divide and rest
instructions: Turn dough out and divide into 8 equal pieces (about 80–90 g each if using a scale).
Shape into tight balls.
Cover and rest 10 minutes to relax gluten for easier rolling.
checks: Dough balls relax and flatten slightly under the towel.

steps.title: Roll into thin rounds
instructions: On a lightly floured surface, roll each dough ball into an 8–9 inch round (about 1/8 inch thick).
Use minimal flour; too much flour dries the surface and reduces flexibility.
Keep rolled rounds covered with a towel while you cook to prevent drying.
checks: Rounds are evenly thin with no thick center.

steps.title: Cook on a hot skillet
instructions: Heat a dry skillet over medium-high until hot (a drop of water should sizzle and evaporate quickly).
Cook one round 45–75 seconds until bubbles form and the underside gets brown spots.
Flip and cook 30–60 seconds until second side has spots. If needed, flip once more for 10–20 seconds.
Adjust heat: lower if burning, raise if pale and stiff.
checks: Soft flatbread with scattered brown spots; not brittle.

steps.title: Steam to keep pliable (critical for wraps)
instructions: As each flatbread finishes, immediately stack it under a clean towel (or in a covered container).
The trapped steam keeps them flexible and wrap-ready.
Let the stack rest 5 minutes before wrapping for best pliability.

steps.title: Warm-and-wrap method (for tight shawarma wraps)
instructions: Re-warm a flatbread 10–15 seconds per side in a hot skillet or 15–20 seconds in the microwave under a damp paper towel.
Spread sauce (toum/tahini) thinly, leaving a 1-inch border.
Add shawarma meat + roasted vegetables + pickles; don’t overfill.
Fold bottom up, fold sides in, then roll tightly. Let seam-side down 30 seconds to seal.
checks: Wrap is tight, sealed, and flexible without splitting.

roomTemp.notes: Cool fully, keep in a sealed bag or container to prevent drying.

fridge.notes: Re-warm briefly on skillet or microwave under damp towel to restore pliability.

freezer.notes: Freeze with parchment between breads. Thaw at room temp, then warm to serve.

reheat.microwave: 15–25 seconds under a damp paper towel
reheat.oven: Wrap stack in foil; 300°F (150°C) for 8–10 minutes

batchNotes: Double batch for freezer stash—flatbreads freeze extremely well.
Keep breads covered at all times; exposed rounds dry and crack when rolled.
If breads are stiff, your heat is too low (they dry out before spotting). Increase heat slightly.

makeAhead.best: Make up to 2 days ahead; re-warm to wrap.
makeAhead.freezer: Freeze same day for best texture.

notes: If you want thicker pita pockets: roll slightly thicker (3/16 inch) and cook hotter to encourage ballooning.
For whole wheat blend: replace up to 1 cup AP flour with whole wheat; add 1–2 tbsp extra water.
Steam-hold (stacking under towel) is the main trick for wrap pliability.
Avoid over-flouring the surface; flour dries the dough and creates brittle breads.
If you need ultra-soft wraps, use the yogurt option and keep the dough on the soft side.
Add 1/4 cup plain yogurt and reduce water by ~2 tbsp at first (add back only if needed).
Result: softer, more forgiving wrap with a slight tang.
Replace yeast + sugar with 2 tsp baking powder.
Skip long rise; rest 15 minutes after mixing, then roll and cook.
Texture is more ‘soft tortilla/naan’ than classic pita-style.
Replace up to 1/3 of the flour with whole wheat.
Add 1–2 tbsp extra water and extend rest after dividing to 15 minutes.
